title: Geometric Deep Learning for Computer-Aided Design: A Survey
abstract: This survey offers a comprehensive overview of learning-based methods in computer-aided design across various categories, including similarity analysis and retrieval, 2D and 3D CAD model synthesis, and point cloud-based CAD reconstruction. It covers geometric deep learning techniques applied to B-rep, mesh, point cloud, and voxel representations.
method_summary: Comprehensive survey of geometric DL methods for CAD covering retrieval, synthesis, reconstruction, and segmentation. Organizes by task and representation type. Most directly comparable existing survey to our work.

title: Large Language Models for Computer-Aided Design: A Survey
abstract: While substantial research has been conducted on LLMs in various fields, a comprehensive review focusing on their integration with Computer-Aided Design (CAD) remains notably absent. CAD is the industry standard for 3D modeling and plays a vital role in the design and development of products across different industries. This survey provides a comprehensive review of LLM applications in CAD.
method_summary: First survey specifically focused on LLM+CAD integration. Covers text-to-CAD, code generation, VLM-based approaches, and LLM-driven design agents. Key comparison target for our Table 1 as it focuses on the LLM dimension we also cover.

title: 3D Shape Generation: A Survey
abstract: This survey provides a comprehensive overview of the current state-of-the-art in 3D shape generation, organizing the discussion around three core components: shape representations, generative modeling techniques, and conditioning mechanisms. It covers point clouds, meshes, implicit functions, and parametric CAD representations.
method_summary: Broad 3D shape generation survey organized by representation and generation paradigm. Covers diffusion, autoregressive, GAN, VAE, and flow-based methods. Overlaps with our Sec 5 but lacks CAD-specific depth on B-rep, sequences, and manufacturing.

title: Advances in 3D Generation: A Survey
abstract: This survey aims to introduce the fundamental methodologies of 3D generation methods and establish a structured roadmap, encompassing 3D representation, generation methods, datasets, and corresponding applications.
method_summary: Broad survey of 3D generation covering NeRF, 3DGS, diffusion-based, and feed-forward methods. Focuses on general 3D content creation rather than engineering CAD. Useful comparison for our Table 1 to show our CAD-specific differentiation.

title: Diffusion Models in 3D Vision: A Survey
abstract: Diffusion models, originally designed for 2D generative tasks, offer the potential for more flexible, probabilistic approaches that can better capture the variability and uncertainty present in real-world 3D data. This survey provides a comprehensive overview of diffusion models applied to 3D vision tasks.
method_summary: Survey of diffusion models for 3D vision covering generation, reconstruction, completion, and editing. Relevant to our Sec 5 (3D generation) but does not address CAD-specific representations or manufacturing constraints.

title: Advancing Additive Manufacturing through Deep Learning: A Comprehensive Review of Current Progress and Future Challenges
abstract: Additive manufacturing (AM) has already proved itself to be the potential alternative to widely-used subtractive manufacturing due to its extraordinary capacity of manufacturing highly customized products. This review covers deep learning applications in AM including defect detection, process monitoring, quality prediction, and design optimization.
method_summary: Comprehensive review of DL in additive manufacturing covering process monitoring, defect detection, quality prediction, and generative design for AM. Relevant comparison for our Sec 8 (manufacturing) and Table 1.

title: A Survey On Text-to-3D Contents Generation In The Wild
abstract: In this survey, we conduct an in-depth investigation of the latest text-to-3D creation methods. We provide a comprehensive background on text-to-3D creation, including discussions on datasets employed, evaluation metrics, and the fundamental generative models utilized.
method_summary: Survey of text-to-3D generation methods covering SDS-based optimization, feed-forward generation, and multi-view approaches. Focuses on general 3D content (NeRF/mesh) rather than parametric CAD. Comparison target for Table 1.

title: Applications of Artificial Intelligence in the AEC Industry: A Review
venue: Journal of Asian Architecture and Building Engineering
abstract: In recent years, with the rapid development of artificial intelligence (AI) technology, AI has been successfully applied across various domains within the construction sector. This paper reviews AI applications in architecture, engineering, and construction including BIM automation, structural design, and construction management.
method_summary: Review of AI in AEC covering BIM automation, generative architectural design, structural optimization, and construction robotics. Relevant for our coverage of AEC domain in Sec 1 and comparison in Table 1.

title: A Survey on Deep Learning in 3D CAD Reconstruction
abstract: This survey reviews deep learning methods for 3D CAD reconstruction from various inputs including point clouds, images, and sketches. It covers methods for B-rep recovery, parametric model inference, and sketch-based modeling.
method_summary: Survey focused specifically on DL-based CAD reconstruction (reverse engineering). Covers point-to-CAD, image-to-CAD, and sketch-to-CAD pipelines. Narrower scope than our survey but directly relevant to Sec 5/6.

title: Geometric Deep Learning: Grids, Groups, Graphs, Geodesics, and Gauges
abstract: The last decade has witnessed an experimental revolution in data science and machine learning, epitomised by deep learning methods. This paper provides a unifying framework for geometric deep learning based on symmetry and invariance principles, covering CNNs, GNNs, Transformers, and equivariant networks.
method_summary: Foundational survey/textbook on geometric deep learning providing unified framework via symmetry principles. Establishes theoretical foundations for GNNs, equivariant networks, and mesh/graph processing that underpin many AI+CAD methods.

title: PointNet: Deep Learning on Point Sets for 3D Classification and Segmentation
abstract: We design a novel type of neural network that directly consumes point clouds and well respects the permutation invariance of points in the input. Our network, named PointNet, provides a unified architecture for applications ranging from object classification, part segmentation, to scene semantic parsing.
method_summary: Foundational architecture for point cloud processing. Introduced permutation-invariant deep learning on unordered point sets via symmetric functions (max pooling). Basis for many subsequent CAD point cloud methods.

title: PointNet++: Deep Hierarchical Feature Learning on Point Sets in a Metric Space
abstract: We introduce a hierarchical neural network that applies PointNet recursively on a nested partitioning of the input point set. By exploiting metric space distances, our network is able to learn local features with increasing contextual scales.
method_summary: Extended PointNet with hierarchical grouping and local feature learning. Set-abstraction layers capture multi-scale geometric features. Widely used as backbone in CAD point cloud processing pipelines.

title: Dynamic Graph CNN for Learning on Point Clouds
abstract: We propose a new neural network module dubbed EdgeConv suitable for CNN-based high-level tasks on point clouds including classification and segmentation. EdgeConv acts on graphs dynamically computed in each layer of the network, capturing local geometric structures while maintaining permutation invariance.
method_summary: Introduced EdgeConv with dynamic k-NN graph construction in feature space. Key innovation: graph is recomputed per layer, enabling semantic neighborhood discovery. Foundational for graph-based 3D learning in CAD.

title: BRepNet: A Topological Message Passing System for Solid Models
abstract: We introduce BRepNet, a neural network architecture designed to operate directly on B-rep data structures, avoiding the need to approximate the model as meshes or point clouds. BRepNet defines convolution on the face-edge-vertex topology using coedge-based message passing.
method_summary: First neural architecture operating directly on B-rep topology. Introduced coedge-centric message passing on the face-edge-vertex graph. Foundational for B-rep learning, enabling segmentation and classification without mesh/point cloud conversion.

title: UV-Net: Learning from Boundary Representations
abstract: We introduce UV-Net, a novel neural network architecture and representation designed to operate directly on Boundary representation (B-rep) data from 3D CAD models. UV-Net combines CNN processing of UV-sampled face grids with GNN processing of the face adjacency graph.
method_summary: Dual-branch architecture for B-rep: CNN on UV-parameterized face grids + GNN on adjacency graph. Demonstrated strong performance on classification and segmentation. Established UV-grid sampling as standard B-rep featurization.

title: DeepCAD: A Deep Generative Network for Computer-Aided Design Models
abstract: We present the first deep generative model for CAD models that directly generates CAD construction sequences. Unlike meshes and point clouds, CAD models encode the user creation process of 3D shapes. Our Transformer-based network generates sequences of sketch-and-extrude operations.
method_summary: Pioneered autoregressive CAD sequence generation via Transformer. Introduced the sketch-and-extrude tokenization scheme (quantized coordinates, operation types) that became the standard for subsequent work. Established the DeepCAD dataset.

title: SkexGen: Autoregressive Generation of CAD Construction Sequences with Disentangled Codebooks
abstract: We present SkexGen, a novel autoregressive generative model for CAD construction sequences containing sketch-and-extrude modeling operations. Our model utilizes distinct Transformer architectures to encode CAD sequences into disentangled codebooks representing different aspects of the design.
method_summary: Advanced CAD sequence generation with disentangled VQ-VAE codebooks separating sketch topology, geometry, and extrusion parameters. Enabled controllable generation by manipulating individual codebooks. Key representation paper for CAD sequences.

title: HNC-CAD: Hierarchical Neural Coding for Controllable CAD Model Generation
abstract: We represent high-level design concepts of a CAD model as a three-level hierarchical tree of neural codes, from global part arrangement down to local curve geometry. Two-stage cascaded auto-regressive transformers learn to generate these codes for controllable CAD model generation.
method_summary: Introduced hierarchical neural coding for CAD: three-level codebook (global arrangement, sketch structure, curve geometry) with cascaded autoregressive generation. Demonstrated fine-grained control over generated CAD models.

title: CSGNet: Neural Shape Parser for Constructive Solid Geometry
abstract: We present a neural architecture that takes as input a 2D or 3D shape and outputs a program that generates the shape. The instructions in our program are based on constructive solid geometry principles, i.e., a set of boolean operations on shape primitives.
method_summary: First neural network to parse shapes into CSG programs. Introduced the shape-to-program paradigm for CSG. Foundational work for neural CSG representation learning.

title: UCSG-Net: Unsupervised Discovering of Constructive Solid Geometry Tree
abstract: We propose UCSG-Net which incorporates a constructive solid geometry framework that represents a shape as a decomposition into primitives. It allows to embody a 3D shape of high complexity and non-convexity with a simple tree representation of Boolean operations, learned without CSG supervision.
method_summary: Unsupervised CSG tree discovery from occupancy grids. Key advance: no ground-truth CSG trees needed for training. Demonstrated that neural networks can discover meaningful CSG decompositions purely from shape supervision.

title: D2CSG: Unsupervised Learning of Compact CSG Trees with Dual Complements and Dropouts
abstract: We present D2CSG, a neural model composed of two dual and complementary network branches, with dropouts, for unsupervised learning of compact constructive solid geometry (CSG) representations of 3D shapes.
method_summary: Advanced unsupervised CSG learning with dual complementary branches and dropout regularization for compact tree generation. Produces more concise CSG programs than prior methods.

title: Generating Constructive Solid Geometry Instead of Meshes by Fine-Tuning a Code-Generation LLM
abstract: This paper introduces a novel approach for the generation of 3D geometry that generates surface-based Constructive Solid Geometry (CSG) by leveraging a code-generation LLM. First, we create a dataset of OpenSCAD programs, then fine-tune a code LLM to generate CSG code from text descriptions.
method_summary: Bridges CSG and LLM paradigms by fine-tuning code-generation LLMs to produce OpenSCAD CSG programs. Demonstrates that LLMs can generate valid CSG code, connecting the code representation to the CSG representation.

title: CAD-Coder: A New Paradigm for CAD Generation with Scalable Large Model Capabilities
abstract: To tackle the limitations of fixed-vocabulary CAD sequence representations, we propose generating CadQuery code directly from text, leveraging the strengths of pretrained LLMs to produce 3D models without intermediate representations, using this Python-based CAD scripting language.
method_summary: Proposes direct CadQuery code generation from text via LLMs, bypassing fixed-vocabulary tokenization. Demonstrates scalability advantages of code representation over sequence representation for CAD generation.

title: Towards Text-based CAD Prototyping via Modality-Specific Tokenization
abstract: Computer-Aided Design (CAD) is a foundational component of industrial prototyping, where models are defined not by raw coordinates but by construction sequences such as sketches and extrusions. This study proposes a modality-specific tokenizer (CAD-Tokenizer) that converts CAD construction sequences into primitive tokens via sequence-based VQ-VAE.
method_summary: Introduces CAD-Tokenizer: modality-aware VQ-VAE that tokenizes sketch and extrusion primitives separately. Enables text-to-CAD via standard language model architectures operating on CAD-specific tokens.

title: BRepFormer: AutoRegressive Generation with B-rep Holistic Token Sequence Representation
abstract: We propose a holistic B-rep token sequence representation that encodes both geometry and topology of B-rep models into a unified token sequence. A face-edge VQ-VAE learns discrete tokens, and an autoregressive Transformer generates complete B-rep models.
method_summary: First autoregressive generation directly in B-rep token space. Introduces holistic tokenization of B-rep geometry and topology into a flat sequence, enabling Transformer-based B-rep generation without intermediate representations.

title: Masked BRep Autoencoder via Hierarchical Graph Transformer
abstract: We introduce a novel self-supervised learning framework that automatically learns representations from input computer-aided design (CAD) models for downstream tasks, including part classification, model retrieval, and segmentation, using masked autoencoding on hierarchical B-rep graph transformers.
method_summary: Self-supervised pre-training for B-rep via masked autoencoding on hierarchical graph transformers. Learns general-purpose B-rep representations transferable to classification, retrieval, and segmentation tasks.

title: OpenECAD: An Efficient Visual Language Model for Editable 3D-CAD Design
abstract: We fine-tuned pre-trained models to create OpenECAD (0.55B, 0.89B, and 4.2B), leveraging the visual, logical, coding, and general capabilities of visual language models. OpenECAD can process images of 3D designs as input and generate highly structured 2D sketches and 3D construction commands.
method_summary: Fine-tuned VLMs for image-to-CAD generation producing editable parametric models. Demonstrates that small VLMs (0.55B-4.2B) can bridge visual understanding and CAD code generation.

title: CadVLM: Bridging Language and Vision in the Generation of Parametric CAD Sketches
abstract: We propose CadVLM, an end-to-end vision language model for CAD generation. Our approach involves adapting pre-trained foundation models to manipulate engineering sketches effectively, integrating visual and textual understanding for parametric CAD sketch generation.
method_summary: End-to-end VLM adapted for parametric CAD sketch generation. Bridges visual understanding of engineering drawings with parametric sketch output. Demonstrates VLM applicability to CAD-specific tasks.

title: A Language Model-Driven Multi-Agent System for Collaborative Design
abstract: We present an approach that mirrors engineering team structure with a Vision Language Model (VLM)-based Multi Agent System, with access to parametric CAD tooling and tool documentation. Combining agents for analysis, design, and verification enables collaborative CAD design.
method_summary: Multi-agent VLM system for collaborative CAD design with specialized agents for analysis, design, and verification. Demonstrates agentic AI architecture for engineering design workflows.

title: Multi-Scale Latent Diffusion with Mamba+ for Complex Parametric Sequence
abstract: We design a novel autoencoder that integrates Mamba+ and Transformer to transfer parameterized CAD sequences into latent representations. The Mamba+ block incorporates a forget gate mechanism for handling long parametric sequences in CAD generation.
method_summary: Combines Mamba state-space model with Transformer for CAD sequence latent diffusion. Addresses long-sequence challenge in parametric CAD generation with multi-scale latent representations.

title: CAD-Coder: Text-to-CAD Generation with Chain-of-Thought and Geometric Reward
abstract: Extensive experiments demonstrate that CAD-Coder enables LLMs to generate diverse, valid, and complex CAD models directly from natural language, advancing the state of the art of text-to-CAD generation through chain-of-thought reasoning and geometric reward signals.
method_summary: Text-to-CAD via LLM with chain-of-thought reasoning and geometric reward (RLHF-style). Demonstrates that structured reasoning improves CAD code generation quality. Relevant to both AI techniques and taxonomy.

title: Iterative Program Editing for CAD Reverse Engineering
abstract: The model outputs a runnable CadQuery Python program, feeding its rendered mesh back to the next step. This iterative program editing approach enables progressive refinement of CAD programs from 3D inputs.
method_summary: Iterative CadQuery program editing for reverse engineering. Generates and refines CAD code through render-compare-edit loops. Demonstrates the code representation's advantage for iterative refinement.

title: Self-Supervised CAD Reconstruction by Learning Sketch-Extrude Operations
abstract: Drawing inspiration from the modeling language most commonly used in modern CAD software, we propose to learn 2D sketches and 3D extrusion parameters from raw shapes, from which a set of extrusion features can be assembled to reconstruct the input shape in a self-supervised manner.
method_summary: Self-supervised learning of sketch-extrude decomposition from raw 3D shapes without CAD sequence supervision. Bridges the gap between geometric inputs and parametric CAD outputs.

title: State Space Model For 3D Computer-Aided Design Generative Modeling
abstract: Recently, the parametric CAD sequence as the design logic of an object has been widely mined by sequence models. However, the industrial CAD models, especially in component objects, are fine-grained and complex. We propose using state space models for efficient CAD sequence generation.
method_summary: Applies state space models (Mamba-family) to CAD sequence generation for handling fine-grained industrial component complexity. Addresses the long-sequence limitation of Transformer-based CAD generation.

title: LLM4CAD: Multi-Modal Large Language Models for 3D Computer-Aided Design Generation
abstract: Multi-Modal Large Language Models for 3D Computer-Aided Design Generation. Proceedings of the ASME 2024 International Design Engineering Technical Conferences.
method_summary: Early work on multi-modal LLMs for CAD generation presented at ASME IDETC. Demonstrates integration of visual and textual modalities for CAD model generation using LLMs.

title: An Open-Source Vision-Language Model for Computer-Aided Design Code Generation
abstract: Efficient creation of accurate and editable 3D CAD models is critical in engineering design, significantly impacting cost and time-to-market in product innovation. Current manual workflows remain highly labor-intensive. We present an open-source VLM for CAD code generation.
method_summary: Open-source VLM specifically trained for CAD code generation from images. Addresses the need for accessible, reproducible VLM-based CAD tools. Relevant to both AI techniques and open-source ecosystem.

title: Intelligent CAD 2.0
abstract: Explores how modern AI and generative techniques can transform computer-aided design. Examines how the current generation of intelligent CAD differs from its predecessor and proposes that ICAD systems should assume an intensional rather than extensional role in the design process, offering a framework for the next generation (ICAD 2.0).
method_summary: Vision paper proposing the ICAD 2.0 paradigm where AI takes an intensional role in design (understanding design intent) rather than extensional (manipulating geometry). Identifies the shift from tool-centric to intent-centric CAD as the key future direction.

title: Mamba-CAD: State Space Model For 3D Computer-Aided Design Generative Modeling
abstract: Addresses generative modeling for complex industrial CAD designs by introducing Mamba-CAD, which handles longer parametric sequences. Combines an encoder-decoder framework based on Mamba architecture with self-supervised pre-training on CAD reconstruction. A GAN produces synthetic representations decoded into parametric sequences. Created a dataset of 77,078 CAD models.
method_summary: Tackles the long-sequence challenge in CAD generation using state space models (Mamba) instead of transformers. Creates a new large-scale dataset with longer sequences, demonstrating that SSMs can better handle the computational demands of industrial-complexity CAD.

title: MamTiff-CAD: Multi-Scale Latent Diffusion with Mamba+ for Complex Parametric Sequence
abstract: Addresses parametric CAD sequence generation by combining Mamba+ and Transformer in an autoencoder to capture long-range dependencies. A diffusion model trained on latent embeddings learns distributions for extended command sequences. Constructed a dataset with sequences up to 256 commands and demonstrated SOTA results on 60-256 command sequence generation.
method_summary: Addresses the long-sequence bottleneck (60-256 commands) in CAD generation by combining Mamba+ with Transformer in a multi-scale latent diffusion framework. Demonstrates that hybrid architectures can handle sequence lengths where pure transformers degrade.

title: GeoFusion-CAD: Structure-Aware Diffusion with Geometric State Space for Parametric 3D Design
abstract: Addresses parametric CAD sequence generation with a diffusion framework encoding programs as hierarchical trees. Uses a lightweight C-Mamba block to model long-range dependencies. Introduces DeepCAD-240, a benchmark extending sequence lengths from 40 to 240 commands. Achieves superior performance on both short and long sequences.
method_summary: Introduces DeepCAD-240 benchmark for long-sequence CAD generation (up to 240 operations) and proposes structure-aware diffusion with geometric state space models. Directly addresses the evaluation gap for complex, multi-operation CAD designs.

title: ArtiCAD: Articulated CAD Assembly Design via Multi-Agent Code Generation
abstract: Introduces a training-free multi-agent system for generating editable, articulated CAD assemblies from text or images. Divides the task among four specialized agents and predicts assembly relationships using a Connector that defines attachment points and joint parameters. Includes validation steps and cross-stage rollback mechanism.
method_summary: Tackles the assembly generation challenge with a multi-agent architecture where specialized agents handle part design, connector definition, and assembly validation. Demonstrates that articulated multi-part CAD remains largely unexplored and requires agent-based decomposition.

title: Agent-Aided Design for Dynamic CAD Models
abstract: Addresses a gap in agentic CAD design systems: their inability to generate complex 3D assemblies with moving parts. Introduces AADvark, which captures dynamic part interactions with one or more degrees-of-freedom to reason about assemblies like pistons and scissors. Combines LLMs with constraint solver tools and specialized visual feedback.
method_summary: Identifies dynamic assembly generation (moving parts with degrees of freedom) as a key unsolved challenge. Proposes agent-aided design with constraint solvers and visual feedback loops for iterative refinement of mechanical assemblies.

title: Aligning Constraint Generation with Design Intent in Parametric CAD
abstract: Addresses constraint generation for CAD sketches using alignment techniques from reasoning LLMs. Tackles the design alignment problem: ensuring generated constraints capture design intent so geometry updates predictably. Achieves fully-constrained 93% of sketches compared to 34% with naive SFT and 8.9% without SFT.
method_summary: Directly addresses the design intent challenge: AI-generated constraints must capture the designer's structural intent, not just geometric accuracy. Uses RLHF-style alignment with constraint solver feedback to bridge the intent gap.

title: cadrille: Multi-modal CAD Reconstruction with Online Reinforcement Learning
abstract: Multi-modal approach to CAD reconstruction that simultaneously processes point clouds, images, and text. Employs a two-stage pipeline combining supervised fine-tuning on synthetic data with reinforcement learning using online feedback. Demonstrates that online RL algorithms such as GRPO outperform offline alternatives.
method_summary: Demonstrates multi-modal CAD reconstruction as a step toward unified CAD foundation models. The use of RL with online feedback addresses the precision gap between AI-generated and ground-truth CAD, a key engineering challenge.

title: BrepCoder: A Unified Multimodal Large Language Model for Multi-task B-rep Reasoning
abstract: Presents a unified multimodal LLM for diverse CAD tasks using B-rep inputs. Converts CAD modeling sequences into Python-like code and aligns them with B-rep. Two-stage training: pre-training on reverse engineering to capture geometric features, then extending to downstream tasks like completion, error correction, and CAD QA.
method_summary: Proposes a unified multi-task CAD foundation model that handles B-rep reasoning across completion, error correction, and QA. Addresses the representation unification challenge by treating B-rep as structural code aligned with LLM capabilities.

title: DreamCAD: Scaling Multi-modal CAD Generation using Differentiable Parametric Surfaces
abstract: Framework for generating editable CAD models from various input modalities without requiring specialized annotations. Leverages parametric surfaces and differentiable tessellation to train on large-scale 3D datasets. Introduces CADCap-1M, the largest CAD captioning dataset to date with 1M+ descriptions.
method_summary: Addresses data scarcity by creating CADCap-1M (1M+ CAD-text pairs) and enabling training on general 3D datasets via differentiable parametric surfaces. Demonstrates scaling as a path toward CAD foundation models.

title: CADEvolve: Creating Realistic CAD via Program Evolution
abstract: Evolution-based pipeline that starts from simple primitives and, via VLM-guided edits and validations, incrementally grows CAD programs toward industrial-grade complexity. Generates 8,000 complex parts as executable CadQuery parametric generators, yielding 1.3 million scripts paired with rendered geometry.
method_summary: Addresses the synthetic data quality gap by evolving CAD programs from simple to industrial-grade complexity using VLM guidance. Produces 1.3M diverse scripts, demonstrating evolutionary approaches to overcome data scarcity and diversity limitations.

title: Learning From Design Procedure To Generate CAD Programs for Data Augmentation
abstract: Addresses challenges in generating CAD programs using LLMs by proposing a data augmentation method. Prompts an LLM to generate CAD programs conditioned on a reference surface program and a modeling procedure inspired by industrial design practices. Enriches geometric diversity with spline-based curvature features.
method_summary: Tackles data diversity challenge by using industrial design procedures as conditioning for LLM-based CAD program generation. Introduces spline-based features typically underrepresented in existing datasets, addressing the distribution gap between academic and industrial CAD.

title: GenCAD-3D: CAD Program Generation using Multimodal Latent Space Alignment and Synthetic Dataset Balancing
abstract: Addresses automated CAD program generation from geometric data like point clouds and meshes. Combines contrastive learning for aligning latent embeddings between CAD and geometric encoders with latent diffusion models. Introduces SynthBal, a synthetic data augmentation approach that balances datasets.
method_summary: Addresses dataset imbalance in CAD through SynthBal synthetic augmentation. Demonstrates that multimodal latent alignment between CAD programs and geometric representations is key to bridging the representation gap.

title: VideoCAD: A Dataset and Model for Learning Long-Horizon 3D CAD UI Interactions from Video
abstract: Introduces VideoCAD, a large-scale synthetic dataset with over 41,000 annotated video recordings of CAD operations. Addresses the gap in UI agent research by focusing on precision engineering tasks with significantly longer time horizons. Proposes VideoCADFormer for learning CAD interactions from video.
method_summary: Creates the first large-scale video dataset of CAD UI interactions (41K videos), enabling AI agents to learn CAD operations by watching. Highlights challenges in precise action grounding, spatial reasoning, and long-horizon dependencies for CAD agents.

title: CAD-Assistant: Tool-Augmented VLLMs as Generic CAD Task Solvers
abstract: Presents a general-purpose CAD agent leveraging a powerful VLLM as a planner and a tool-augmentation paradigm using CAD-specific tools. Processes multimodal queries by generating actions executed on FreeCAD via its Python API. Incorporates specialized tools including sketch parameterization, rendering, and cross-section generation.
method_summary: Proposes the tool-augmented VLLM paradigm for CAD: an AI agent that plans with vision-language models and executes via CAD software APIs. Demonstrates the agent-as-CAD-copilot architecture as a near-term future direction.

title: Toward AI-driven Multimodal Interfaces for Industrial CAD Modeling
abstract: Examines how AI-driven multimodal interfaces can revolutionize industrial 3D CAD modeling by improving workflow efficiency and user experience. Addresses integration challenges including software constraints and user adoption barriers, analyzing Bayesian workflow inference, multimodal input strategies, and collaborative AI systems.
method_summary: Identifies key engineering challenges for AI-CAD integration: software API constraints, user adoption barriers, and the gap between research prototypes and industrial deployment. Proposes multimodal interface design principles for human-AI collaborative CAD.

title: neuralCAD-Edit: An Expert Benchmark for Multimodal-Instructed 3D CAD Model Editing
abstract: Benchmark for editing 3D CAD models created by expert engineers. Captured videos of professional designers working with CAD software including speech, gestures, and annotations. Even the best foundation model (GPT 5.2) scores 53% lower (absolute) than CAD experts in human acceptance trials.
method_summary: Reveals the massive performance gap between foundation models and human CAD experts on editing tasks (53% absolute gap). Establishes that multimodal instruction understanding for CAD editing remains a major unsolved challenge.

title: Human-in-the-Loop: Quantitative Evaluation of 3D Models Generation by Large Language Models
abstract: Framework for evaluating LLM-generated 3D models using quantitative metrics like volumetric accuracy and surface alignment. Tests with an L bracket component across four input modalities. Code-level prompts achieve perfect reconstruction across all metrics.
method_summary: Proposes standardized quantitative evaluation metrics for LLM-generated CAD models. Identifies the evaluation gap: current visual inspection methods are insufficient, and standardized benchmarks with geometric metrics are needed.

title: 3DGen-Bench: Comprehensive Benchmark Suite for 3D Generative Models
abstract: Addresses the gap between rapid 3D generation advances and slower evaluation development. Created 3DGen-Arena for collecting human preferences and 3DGen-Bench, a large-scale multi-dimension human preference dataset. Trained evaluation models that unify quality evaluation of text-to-3D and image-to-3D generation.
method_summary: Addresses the benchmark standardization challenge for 3D generation. Proposes human-preference-aligned evaluation models that correlate better with human judgment than existing metrics, establishing evaluation infrastructure for the field.

title: The Dawn of Agentic EDA: A Survey of Autonomous Digital Chip Design
abstract: Addresses the semiconductor industry's Productivity Gap by examining the shift from AI for EDA optimization toward Agentic EDA as a Constrained Neuro-Symbolic Optimization problem. Proposes a Cognitive Stack framework (Perception, Cognition, Action) and advocates for Sim-to-Silicon benchmarks and formal grounding.
method_summary: Parallel survey in chip design that maps the evolution from AI-assisted to fully autonomous design agents. The Cognitive Stack framework and Sim-to-Silicon benchmark concept are directly transferable to mechanical CAD's future trajectory.

title: CADFusion: Text-to-CAD Generation Through Infusing Visual Feedback in Large Language Models
abstract: Framework leveraging LLMs to convert text into CAD parametric sequences. Alternates between sequential learning using ground-truth parametric data and visual feedback that rewards sequences rendering into preferred objects. The two stages alternate throughout training ensuring balanced learning.
method_summary: Demonstrates the visual feedback loop as essential for bridging the gap between text understanding and geometric precision in CAD generation. The alternating SL/VF training paradigm addresses the fundamental challenge of grounding language in 3D geometry.

title: From Intent to Execution: Multimodal Chain-of-Thought Reinforcement Learning for Precise CAD Code Generation
abstract: Introduces CAD-RL combining chain-of-thought reasoning with RL to generate executable CAD code from natural language. Uses three task-specific rewards: executability, geometric accuracy, and external evaluation. Releases ExeCAD dataset of 16,540 real-world CAD examples.
method_summary: Addresses the intent-to-execution gap in CAD generation through multimodal CoT reasoning and RL. The three-reward framework (executability, accuracy, evaluation) directly tackles the engineering precision challenge.

title: CAD-GPT: Synthesising CAD Construction Sequence with Spatial Reasoning-Enhanced Multimodal LLMs
abstract: Method for generating CAD models from images or text descriptions. Introduces a 3D Modeling Spatial Mechanism that maps spatial positions and rotation angles into linguistic features, enabling precise determination of sketch orientation and positioning.
method_summary: Addresses the spatial reasoning challenge in CAD generation by mapping 3D spatial information into linguistic features that LLMs can process. Demonstrates that spatial reasoning enhancement is critical for precise CAD synthesis.

title: CADmium: Fine-Tuning Code Language Models for Text-Driven Sequential CAD Design
abstract: Presents a dataset of over 170k CAD models with human-like descriptions and fine-tunes code language models to generate CAD sequences from natural language. Introduces geometric and topological metrics based on sphericity, mean curvature, and Euler characteristic.
method_summary: Creates a large-scale text-CAD paired dataset (170K models) and proposes geometric/topological evaluation metrics. Addresses both data scarcity and evaluation standardization challenges simultaneously.

title: QueryCAD: Grounded Question Answering for CAD Models
abstract: System for extracting information from CAD models using natural language. Includes SegCAD, an open-vocabulary instance segmentation model for identifying specific parts. Presents a benchmark for evaluation and demonstrates application in automatic robot program synthesis.
method_summary: Addresses the CAD understanding challenge: enabling natural language queries over CAD models for downstream tasks like robot programming. Highlights the gap between CAD as static geometry and CAD as queryable design knowledge.

title: RLCAD: Reinforcement Learning Training Gym for Revolution Involved CAD Command Sequence Generation
abstract: Introduces a reinforcement learning environment for generating CAD command sequences from B-rep geometry. Supports operations beyond sketches, Boolean, and extrusion, including revolution operations. Uses a policy network with geometric engine rewards.
method_summary: Addresses the operation coverage gap: most CAD generation methods only handle sketch+extrude, but real CAD requires revolution, sweep, loft, etc. Proposes RL-based approach to expand the operation vocabulary.

title: CADDreamer: CAD Object Generation from Single-view Images
abstract: Method for generating B-rep CAD objects from single images using a primitive-aware multi-view diffusion model that captures both local geometric details and high-level structural semantics. Encodes primitive semantics into color channels to leverage diffusion model priors.
method_summary: Bridges the gap between image understanding and CAD B-rep generation. Demonstrates that primitive-aware representations can produce compact, structured, edge-sharp, watertight CAD models from single views.
